rulings.Case Title: CHARLES LI VS. MEI LING FANG ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 3, 2023 line 1. PLAINTIFF CHARLES LI'S MOTION FOR RECONSIDERATION is OFF CALENDAR, re-noticed for March 10, 2023. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: MARK SMITH VS. JONES MEMORIAL HOMES INC
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants Amended Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Plaintiffs First Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 3, 2023 line 2. DEFENDANTS' AMENDED NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO STRIKE PLAINTIFF'S FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT is GRANTED. Causes of action for Negligent Infliction of Emotional Distress, Intentional Infliction of Emotional Distress, Negligence and Negligence per Se, self-help eviction, negligence and breach of covenant of quiet enjoyment are stricken from the First Amended Complaint. Plaintiff's time to amend those causes of action lapsed in April 2022. Plaintiff failed to seek leave to amend before filing Fist Amended Complaint on December 20, 2023. Defendant's request for dismissal raised for the reply is not considered. Any such request shall be made a properly noticed motion. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: HUI QIN WU ET AL VS. FEI SU ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 3, 2023 line 4. PLAINTIFFS' MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT / ADJUDICATION is DENIED. Triable issues of fact exist, which include whether the terms of tenancy have been changed (Wi-Fi), relocation payment for Diwei Su, etc. The motion for summary adjudication is GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. The Motion is granted as to the 9th, 11th , 17th , 19th, 20th, 22nd, 26th , 27th. No opposition filed. The Motion is GRANTED as to the 1st, 5th, 13th, 14th, 15th, 16th, 18th, and 24th. Moving party shifted their burden and opposing party failed to create a triable issue of fact. Motion is DENIED as to affirmative defenses 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 8, 10, 21, 23, and 25. These affirmative defenses are properly stated and triable issues of fact exist regarding compliance with applicable provisions of Ellis Act and SFRO. Defendant's untimely CCP 170.6 challenge to the Honorable Charles F. Haines is denied per March 2, 2023 Order. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
